name: Stueckelberg mass mechanism for Abelian gauge bosons
summary: Stueckelberg (1938) showed how to give a U(1) gauge boson a mass without spontaneous symmetry breaking by introducing a Stueckelberg scalar sigma transforming non-linearly: A_mu -> A_mu + d_mu Lambda, sigma -> sigma - m Lambda. The combination D_mu sigma = d_mu sigma + m A_mu is gauge-invariant and gives the boson mass when sigma is gauge-fixed away. Unlike the Higgs mechanism, requires no physical scalar (the Stueckelberg field is purely a longitudinal mode). Renormalizable for Abelian groups; non-renormalizable for non-Abelian (resurrected only as effective theory). Standard in Z' / dark-photon model-building and in dark-sector hidden U(1)'s.

addresses_puzzles.note: Standard way to give mass to dark-photon mediators or Z' bosons in dark-sector model-building without requiring a hidden Higgs. Stueckelberg mass appears in many string-theory dark-sector compactifications naturally.

new_fields: Stueckelberg scalar sigma (gauge artifact, not a physical d.o.f.)
introduces.note: Single new parameter (the gauge-boson mass). The Stueckelberg scalar is not physical - it can be gauged away in unitary gauge.

history: Stueckelberg (1938), 'Die Wechselwirkungskrafte in der Elektrodynamik und in der Feldtheorie der Kernkrafte', Helv. Phys. Acta 11, 225. Modern revival in Z'/dark-photon model-building: Kors-Nath (2004), 'A Stueckelberg extension of the standard model', PLB 586, 366, arXiv:hep-ph/0402047. Modern review: Ruegg, Ruiz-Altaba (2003), 'The Stueckelberg field', Int. J. Mod. Phys. A 19, 3265, arXiv:hep-th/0304245.

references.citation: Ruegg and Ruiz-Altaba, 'The Stueckelberg field', Int. J. Mod. Phys. A 19, 3265 (2004)
Kors and Nath, 'A Stueckelberg extension of the standard model', Phys. Lett. B 586, 366 (2004)
